post.summary: Run the system message delivery diagnostic.
post.description:
Publishes a system message, waits for the production message consumer to
write a cache marker, and reports whether the marker appeared within the
bounded diagnostic window.

post.summary: Run the default Fast LLM key diagnostic.
post.description:
Sends a short bounded prompt to the app-level default Fast LLM client and
returns sanitized success or failure metadata. Successful responses include
the returned joke text, which callers must treat as untrusted plain text.

post.summary: Run the snippet embedding key diagnostic.
post.description:
Sends a short bounded probe text to the Query-profile snippet embedding client
and returns sanitized success or failure metadata. Reports a non-throwing
"not configured" result (rather than calling the provider) when
AppSettings:Llm:Embeddings:Enabled is false, the API key is unset, or the key
still holds its appsettings.json placeholder value.

post.summary: Manually trigger a public source's repository sync.
post.description:
Queues an immediate sync of a public repository source, outside its normal
schedule. System-admin only.

Returns `404` when the source doesn't exist, `409` when a sync is already
queued or running, and `429` when more than 5 manual triggers have been
requested in the last hour.

get.description:
Returns the active confidential OAuth client credentials owned by the
authenticated user in the route organization. Each entry includes the `client_id` and descriptive
metadata; the `client_secret` is never returned by this endpoint.

Requires a signed-in browser session.

post.description:
Provisions a new confidential OAuth client owned by the authenticated user.
Unlike Dynamic Client Registration (which stays public/PKCE-only), this
creates a secret-bearing client for a user who has already signed in with an
external identity provider in the route organization.

The response carries the `client_id` and metadata. The generated
`client_secret` is shown only at creation time and cannot be recovered later,
so it must be stored by the caller.

delete.description:
Deletes the confidential OAuth client identified by `clientId`.
The credential can no longer issue new tokens. Already-issued self-contained
access tokens remain valid until their normal expiry window.

Only credentials owned by the authenticated user can be deleted.

get.description:
Returns the authenticated caller's identity for the current session: profile
claims (name, email, picture, provider) plus the active organization, team,
and slug decoded from the encrypted, server-issued session token.

The caller's role, full organization list, and pending invitations are
resolved fresh from the membership store on each request rather than read
from the token, so role and membership changes take effect immediately.
Requires a signed-in browser session.

Returns the active long-lived bearer tokens owned by the authenticated user.
Each entry exposes only metadata for the route organization (id, name, timestamps); the token value
itself is not stored in recoverable form and is never returned here.

Requires a signed-in browser session.

post.description:
Issues a new long-lived bearer token for the authenticated user, suitable for
scripting and CLI access in place of an interactive session in the route organization.

The plaintext token value is returned **only** in this creation response and
cannot be retrieved again; only metadata is persisted for later list and
delete operations, so the caller must capture the value now.

delete.description:
Deletes the long-lived bearer token metadata identified by `id`. The token stops
authenticating on subsequent requests because validation requires a metadata row.

Only tokens owned by the authenticated user can be deleted.

get.description:
Issues a redirect to GitHub's App installation flow so the current
organization can be connected to the Zeeq GitHub App. A signed `state`
value ties the resulting callback back to this org.

The caller must be authenticated and hold the `owner` or `admin` role in the
active organization.

Landing endpoint GitHub redirects the user back to after they approve (or
cancel) the App installation. It validates the signed `state`, records the
`installation_id` against the originating organization, and redirects the
browser back into the app.

Anonymous by design because GitHub drives the redirect; trust comes from the
signed `state` rather than the session.

Returns every repository the linked GitHub App installation can see, each
annotated with whether it already has a Zeeq repository row and whether it
should appear as a private library source. This list reflects GitHub App
installation access; it is broader than webhook-enabled repositories.

Requires a connected GitHub App installation and the `owner` or `admin` role.

post.description:
Maps an installation-visible GitHub repository into the route organization so
its pull-request and comment webhooks are routed to Zeeq code review instead
of being dropped as a no-op when `Enabled` is true. This does not install the
GitHub App or change GitHub-side repository access.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role.

put.description:
Updates whether an installation-visible GitHub repository appears as a
private source option when creating a library. This is independent from
`Enabled`: hiding a repository does not pause webhook-triggered code-review
work, and showing a repository does not enable webhook processing.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role.

Updates the Zeeq-local settings for an already-configured repository mapping,
identified by `repositoryId`. `Enabled` controls webhook-triggered
code-review work; it does not control GitHub App installation access or
library-source visibility.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role.

delete.description:
Disables the repository mapping identified by `repositoryId`, so its webhooks
are once again ignored by Zeeq. The GitHub App installation is left in place;
only the Zeeq-side routing is turned off.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role.

get.description:
Returns the GitHub repositories already registered in Zeeq for the route
organization, along with their local settings. `Enabled` controls whether
incoming PR and comment webhooks create code-review work. Library-source
visibility is tracked separately and does not change GitHub App access.

Requires active membership in the route organization.

Returns the caller's saved carts ordered newest-saved first. Each cart includes
only lightweight item summaries — hash, title, facet, summary, criticality, and
annotation — not full finding body or card payloads. Safe to call at application
startup. Scoped to the caller's active organization.

post.description:
Persists a client-built draft cart for the first time. The request carries the
client-generated id, generated name, original draft creation timestamp, and all
finding snapshots. Both lightweight summaries (for list UI) and full payloads
(for text/MCP compilation) are stored.

Enforces a 5 saved-cart limit per owner (returns 409 Conflict when reached)
and a 10-item limit per cart (returns 400 Bad Request when exceeded). Saved
carts are immutable server-side — there is no update or patch endpoint; editing
a saved cart is done client-side via copy-to-new-draft followed by delete.

delete.description:
Removes a saved cart by id. Scoped to the caller's active organization and
owner identity — carts owned by other users in the same organization cannot be
deleted by this caller. Returns 404 if the cart does not exist or is not owned
by the caller.

get.description:
Compiles a saved cart's full findings payload into the agent-ready
<instruction_for_agents> XML block. Each finding includes
repo/PR provenance, reviewer facet and agent, the finding body as CDATA, and
the optional per-item annotation as a note attribute. Uses the same builder as
the get_cart_findings MCP tool. Only returns text for saved (server-persisted)
carts. The caller is expected to paste this text into a local agent chat or
pass it to an MCP session.

get.summary: Copy a saved cart to a new draft.
get.description:
Returns the full item payload of a saved cart, including finding bodies, repo/PR
provenance, and annotations. Intended solely for the client-side "Copy to new
draft" action — the client creates a new local draft with a fresh ID and
generated name from this payload. Saved carts are never edited in place: users
copy to a new draft, make changes locally, then save that as a new cart and
optionally delete the original.

Returns a cursor-paginated page of recent pull requests in the organization's
code-review inbox. Results can be narrowed by `teamId`, `repositoryId`, and
`claimStatus`. Pass the cursor fields from the previous page
(`cursorCreatedAtUtc`, `cursorId`) to fetch the next page.

Scoped to the organization identified by the route `orgId`.

get.summary: Find a pull request by provider number.
get.description:
Resolves a single pull request by its repo-scoped provider number
(e.g. GitHub PR #42) within the route `orgId`. Both `repositoryId` and
`pullRequestNumber` are required because PR numbers are only unique within
a repository. Returns the resolved PR detail row so the frontend can
inject it into the inbox without a separate lookup round-trip.

Returns the detail view for a single pull request identified by
`pullRequestRecordId` within the route `orgId`. The `c` query value is a compact,
URL-safe token encoding the record's partition timestamp; it is carried on every
PR DTO as `singleViewToken`. A malformed token yields a 400 `invalid_token`;
an omitted token yields a 400 `missing_token`.

Returns the cursor-paginated review history for one pull request — each
automated or manually requested review run recorded against
`pullRequestRecordId`, newest first.

Scoped to the route `orgId`; pass `createdAtUtc` to pin the parent PR's partition and
the cursor fields to page.

Returns the parsed findings produced by a single review run, identified by
`codeReviewRecordId` within the route `orgId`. This is the detailed per-review payload
(individual findings) behind a row from the review history list.

Pass `createdAtUtc` to locate the record's partition directly.

Returns one review row plus the related set to display in the single-review view.
The `c` query value is a compact, URL-safe token (built by the backend) that packs
the record's partition timestamp with the view mode: agent mode returns the agent
session's reviews, pr mode returns the parent PR's review history. Scoped to the
route `orgId`. A malformed token yields a 400 `invalid_token`; an omitted
token yields a 400 `missing_token`.

post.description:
Manually queues a new review for an existing pull request through the same
durable workflow that webhooks use. Because the user asked explicitly, it
bypasses the draft and webhook-action gates, but still enforces repository
visibility, closed-PR, budget, and active-review guards.

Scoped to the route `orgId`; pass `createdAtUtc` to target the PR's partition.

get.description:
Returns the organization-wide code-review execution settings for the route `orgId` —
the defaults that govern how reviews run across every repository in the org.

put.description:
Replaces the organization-wide code-review execution settings for the route `orgId`.
These defaults apply to every repository unless overridden by a
repository-level configuration.

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

get.description:
Returns the reviewer agents configured for the repository (`repositoryId`)
within the route `orgId`. Reviewer agents are the persisted personas/configurations that
perform automated reviews for that repository.

post.description:
Adds a new reviewer agent to the repository (`repositoryId`) within the route `orgId`.
The agent becomes eligible to run on subsequent reviews for that repository.

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

get.description:
Returns the built-in, clonable reviewer-agent templates within the route `orgId`.
Templates are code-defined starting points (personas) used to seed a new agent's
configuration; they are not persisted and are never tied to a specific repository.

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

put.description:
Updates the reviewer agent identified by `agentId` on repository
`repositoryId` within the route `orgId`. Changes apply to reviews that run after the
update.

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

delete.description:
Disables the reviewer agent identified by `agentId` on repository
`repositoryId` within the route `orgId` so it no longer participates in future reviews.
Past reviews it produced are retained.

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

get.description:
Returns a lightweight, cursor-paginated feed of recent review updates for the
inbox, designed for frequent polling. Filter by `teamId`, `repositoryId`, and
`scope`, and use `reviewCreatedAtLowerBoundUtc` to bound how far back to look.

Each payload is intentionally minimal (status/timestamps) so clients can detect
changes cheaply and fetch full detail only when needed. Scoped to the route `orgId`.

Returns the repository-level code-review configuration for `repositoryId`
within the route `orgId`. These per-repository settings layer on top of the
organization defaults.

put.description:
Replaces the repository-level code-review configuration for `repositoryId`
within the route `orgId`, overriding the organization defaults for this repository only.

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

post.description:
Clears a blocking Zeeq code review check run on the specified pull request.
Any authenticated member of the organization (`orgId`) may bypass the check.

Returns `200` with `cleared: true` when the check was cleared, `200` with
`cleared: false` when no blocking check existed, or `404` when the PR
cannot be found.

get.description: Returns the document metadata rows for the route library.

put.description: Creates or replaces a markdown document in the route library.

delete.description: Deletes one document from the route library by path.

get.description: Returns the full markdown body for one document by path.

Shows the title, keywords, headings, and snippets the indexing pipeline
would extract from a document without persisting changes.
Searches documents in the route library by keyword query.

post.description:
Sets or clears a document's code-review exclusion. Excluded documents do
not surface to code-review agents via list/search tools, but direct reads
by path still resolve. Synced documents are rejected.
Moves one document to a new path within the route library.

get.description: Returns all document libraries in the caller's active organization, ordered by name.

post.description:
Creates a new document library in the caller's active organization.

The name must be unique within the organization and contain only letters, numbers,
hyphens, and underscores — it doubles as the URL segment used to address the library
in subsequent requests.

Pass `source` to create a repository-sourced library instead of a plain
hand-authored one. `source.kind = Public` requires `source.repoUrl` (a raw
`https://github.com/owner/repo` clone URL); `source.kind = Private` requires
`source.repositoryId`, an id from this organization's configured GitHub
repositories. Returns `404` when `source.repositoryId` doesn't resolve to a
configured repository. Creating a repository-sourced library does not queue its
initial sync — call `POST .../libraries/{name}/ingest-run` immediately after.

get.description: Returns the library identified by `name` in the caller's active organization.

put.description:
Updates the name or description of an existing library identified by `name`.

Renaming a library changes the URL segment used to address it; subsequent requests
must use the new name.

delete.description:
Permanently deletes the library and all its documents. Also removes this library's
ID from any repository mappings that referenced it. This operation cannot be undone.

get.description:
Exports hand-authored local documents from the library. `format=zeeq` returns a
signed `.zeeq-export` wrapper that Zeeq can import. `format=zip` returns a
standard zip archive for external use and is not importable by Zeeq.

post.description:
Verifies a `.zeeq-export` file before opening its internal package, then returns
the new, duplicate, and blocked paths without writing any documents.

Imports verified `.zeeq-export` documents into the target library. Duplicate local
paths require `overwriteDuplicates=true`; synced/remote path collisions are blocked.

put.description:
Replaces the full set of repositories mapped to this library. Repositories whose ID
appears in `repositoryIds` gain this library in their reviewer-tool context; all
others lose it. Pass an empty array to clear all mappings.

All IDs must belong to the caller's organization; an unknown or cross-organization
ID returns 400.

get.description:
Searches section or code snippets in the route library. The handler validates
`kind`, resolves the library, embeds the query with full-text fallback, and
returns ranked rows with score components and degraded-mode metadata.

post.summary: Manually trigger a library's repository sync.
post.description:
Queues an immediate sync of the private repository mapped to this library,
outside its normal schedule. Requires the library to be configured with a
private repository source (`sourceKind`/`sourceRepoUrl` set).

Returns `404` when the library doesn't exist, `400` when the library has no
linked repository, `409` when a sync is already queued or running for this
library, and `429` when more than 5 manual triggers have been requested in
the last hour.

post.summary: Clear a stuck private library repository sync.
post.description:
Clears a private repository library's queued/running sync state and makes it
eligible to sync again immediately. If the active run row exists and is still
running, it is marked `Stalled`. Public-source-backed libraries are not reset
through this organization-scoped endpoint.

get.summary: List a library's ingest run history.
get.description:
Returns ingest run history for a repository-sourced library, newest first,
cursor-paginated (`limit` defaults to 10, capped at 50). Pass `nextCursor`
from the previous page's response as `cursor` to fetch the next page; omit
`cursor` for the first page. For a public-source library, this is the
source's shared history — every subscribing library's run history is the
same rows.

Returns `404` when the library doesn't exist, `400` when the library has no
repository source (nothing to list) or `cursor` is malformed.

Returns the organization's LLM configuration for `orgId`. Every member sees the
high-level routing state (whether the org is configured and usable); owners and
admins additionally receive the full tier settings and API-key metadata.

Key metadata never includes plaintext key material.

put.description:
Updates the organization's LLM tier settings for `orgId` — the provider, model,
and key selections that route Zeeq's Fast and other model tiers.

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

post.description:
Stores a provider API key for the organization (`orgId`). The plaintext key is
**write-only**: it is encrypted at rest and can never be read back through the
API — subsequent responses expose only metadata.

Returns `201 Created` with the new key's metadata. Restricted to organization
owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

patch.description:
Updates the display name of an active stored key (`keyId`) for organization
`orgId`. Only the label changes; the encrypted key material is untouched.

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

put.description:
Replaces the secret behind an existing key (`keyId`) for organization `orgId`
with a new plaintext value, keeping the same key identity and references. Like
creation, the supplied plaintext is write-only and encrypted at rest.

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

delete.description:
Disables the stored key (`keyId`) for organization `orgId`. The key must not be
referenced by the active tier settings; reassign those settings first, or the
request is rejected (`400`).

Restricted to organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

post.description:
Runs a bounded, live access check against a provider/model/key combination for
organization `orgId` and reports whether Zeeq can reach it. Useful for
validating a key before saving it into the tier settings.

Output is sanitized so provider errors never leak secrets. Restricted to
organization owners and admins (`403` otherwise).

get.description:
Returns the pending organization invitations addressed to the authenticated
user's email — the invites they can accept or decline. Resolved by email, so
invitations created before the user signed up still appear once they do.

Returns organization and owner display details for a same-domain invitation
addressed to the authenticated user.

post.description:
Accepts the pending invitation (`id`) addressed to the authenticated user,
creating their membership in the inviting organization at the invited role.

The user can only act on invitations addressed to their own email. This route
intentionally remains outside current-org activation filtering so invited
users can join an active org even when their current org is inactive or unset.

Accepts the pending invitation (`id`) addressed to the authenticated user,
then makes the invited organization the user's default organization.

This is used by same-domain onboarding so the user lands in the team they
joined after accepting.

Declines the pending invitation (`id`) addressed to the authenticated user. No
membership is created and the invitation is removed from their pending list.

The user can only act on invitations addressed to their own email. This route
intentionally remains outside current-org activation filtering so users can
clear stale invitations even when their current org is inactive or unset.

Switches the authenticated user's active organization to `orgId` and re-issues
the session cookie with the new org, team, slug, and role claims. Subsequent
requests are then scoped to the newly active organization.

Returns `404` if the user is not a member of `orgId`.

delete.description:
Removes the authenticated user's own membership in `orgId`. The sole remaining
owner cannot leave — ownership must first be transferred to another member —
so an organization is never left without an owner.

put.description:
Marks `orgId` as the authenticated user's default organization — the one their
session starts in at sign-in. Does not change the currently active organization
for this session; use switch-org for that.

get.description:
Returns every organization the authenticated user is a member of, with the
user's role in each. This is the set the user can switch between as their
active organization.

post.description:
Creates a new organization with the authenticated user as its first `owner`,
provisioning the root team alongside it. The chosen slug must be available
across all organizations.

get.description:
Reports whether the given `slug` is free to use for an organization. Pass
`excludeOrgId` when editing an existing organization so its own current slug is
not counted as a conflict.

Intended for live validation while creating or renaming an organization.

Returns the details (name, slug, icon, and related metadata) for the
organization identified by `orgId`. The authenticated user must be a member.

put.description:
Updates the editable profile fields — name, slug, or icon — of the organization
identified by `orgId`. A changed slug must still be available.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role in the organization.

Enables or disables automatic same-domain invitations for the organization.
Enabling derives the claimable domain from the organization creator's email.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role in the organization.

get.description:
Returns the active members of the organization (`orgId`) along with each
member's role. Pending invitees are not included here — see the invitations
endpoints for those.

put.description:
Sets the role of the member (`userId`) within the organization (`orgId`) to the
role in the request body.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role in the organization.

delete.description:
Removes the member (`userId`) from the organization (`orgId`), revoking their
access. To remove yourself, use the leave-organization endpoint instead.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role in the organization.

get.description:
Returns the still-pending invitations that have been sent for the organization
(`orgId`) — invitees who have not yet accepted or declined.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role in the organization.

post.description:
Creates a pending invitation to the organization (`orgId`) for the email in the
request, at the requested role. The invitee sees it under their own
`me/invitations` and joins once they accept.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role in the organization.

delete.description:
Withdraws a pending invitation (`id`) previously sent for the organization
(`orgId`), before the invitee has acted on it. The invitation disappears from
the invitee's pending list.

Requires the `owner` or `admin` role in the organization.

get.description: Returns a bucketed metric_value series for the window, optionally grouped and filtered.

get.summary: Bucketed metric series grouped by two dimensions.
get.description: Returns a bucketed metric_value series for the window, grouped by two distinct dimensions and optionally filtered.
Returns per-bucket p50/p95/p99 for a histogram metric.
Returns recent raw samples for a duration-vs-tokens scatter.
Returns the top-N most-read document paths across section and snippet reads.

get.summary: Top-N read sections or snippets (path + heading), one kind at a time.
get.description:
Returns the top-N most-read sections or code snippets (per `kind`), keyed by document path + heading.

Distinct sections within the same document rank separately.

Section and snippet reads are never combined in one query: a prose section and a code snippet under the same heading share an identical (path, heading) pair.

Combining kinds would merge their counts.
Returns bucketed code-review volume, optionally grouped by repo/author/origin.
Returns bucketed finding-severity sums, optionally grouped by repo/author.
Returns the headline overview numbers for the window.
Returns distinct users, tools, repositories, and authors for the dashboard filters.

post.description:
Accepts first-party JSON agent telemetry and sends it through the
same raw ingest, filtering, and processing path used by OTLP logs.
The authenticated principal determines user and organization scope.

AdminDiagnosticResponse.description: Shared response returned by admin diagnostics.

AdminLlmDiagnosticResponse.description: Response returned by the admin LLM diagnostic.

AdminLlmEmbeddingDiagnosticResponse.description: Response returned by the admin snippet-embedding key diagnostic.

AgentEventKind.description: Event kinds accepted by the JSON telemetry import endpoint.

conversation_id.description: Stable conversation identifier assigned by the source harness.

harness_name.description: Source harness name, such as `codex` or `claude-code`.

events.description: Telemetry events to import for the conversation.

repository_remote_url.description: Optional Git remote URL for the working repository.

head_branch.description: Optional Git branch active when the events were captured.

head_sha.description: Optional Git commit SHA active when the events were captured.

AgentTelemetryImportRequest.description: Request body for importing agent telemetry events as JSON.

eventsAccepted.description: Number of events accepted by the shared ingest path.

AgentTelemetryImportResponse.description: Response returned after imported events are accepted for telemetry processing.

pullRequestNumber.description: Provider PR number that was targeted.

cleared.description: True when the blocking check was cleared.

BypassCheckRunResponse.description: Response for a check-run bypass operation.

id.description: Client-generated cart id, preserved from the draft.

items.description: Full finding payloads to seed the new draft.

CartCopySourceResponse.description: Full saved cart contents returned only for explicit client-side copy-to-new-draft.

CartError.description: Error response for cart API validation failures.

hash.description: Client-computed SHA-256 hex digest of the finding's content.

facet.description: Reviewer facet label, such as Security or Performance.

criticality.description: Finding criticality/severity, as its string name.

annotation.description: Optional free-text note the user attached when adding this finding.

CartFindingResponse.description:
Lightweight per-finding row.  Only these fields are returned by `GET /carts` —
the full finding body is never exposed through the startup list endpoint.

items.description: The caller's saved carts, each with summary-only findings.

CartListResponse.description: List of the caller's saved carts with metadata-only items.

id.description: Client-generated cart id, preserved from the draft.

itemCount.description: Number of findings in the cart.

createdAtUtc.description: When the draft was originally created client-side.

savedAtUtc.description: When the initial save request was processed.

updatedAtUtc.description: When the cart was last changed server-side.

items.description: Lightweight per-finding rows for startup/list UI.

text.description: The compiled agent-instructions text, ready to paste.

ChangeMemberRoleRequest.description: Request body for changing a member's organization role.

clientId.description: OpenIddict client ID to use at the token endpoint.

clientSecret.description: Generated secret shown once to the creating user.

createdAtUtc.description: Timestamp when the credential was created.

scope.description: Required OAuth scope for MCP tool access.

resource.description: RFC 8707 resource indicator for the local MCP resource.

sampleCurl.description: Diagnostic curl command for local setup and copy/paste testing.

ClientCredentialCreated.description: One-time creation response containing the generated client secret.

displayName.description: User-facing name shown in the credential management UI.

ClientCredentialCreateRequest.description: Browser request body for creating a user-owned OAuth client credential.

clientId.description: OpenIddict client ID and local credential row ID.

createdAtUtc.description: Timestamp when the credential was created.

revokedAtUtc.description: Timestamp when the credential was revoked, if any.

ClientCredentialSummary.description: Non-sensitive credential metadata returned by list endpoints.

blockOnCritical.description: Block when the review has at least one Critical finding.

blockOnMajor.description: Block when the review has at least one Major finding. Implies Critical.

CodeReviewCheckRunConfigurationDto.description: Check-run gating settings for a repository.

message.description: Human-readable description of the validation failure.

CodeReviewEndpointError.description: Error response for code-review API validation failures.

includedFiles.description: Allowlist rules. Empty means all repository-scoped files can activate the agent.

excludedFiles.description: Deny rules that always win over includes.

CodeReviewerActivationConfigurationDto.description: File activation rules for one persisted reviewer agent.

createdAtUtc.description: Timestamp when the agent configuration was created.

updatedAtUtc.description: Most recent timestamp when the agent configuration changed.

repositoryId.description: Repository mapping this agent belongs to.

teamId.description: Team context inherited from the repository mapping, when any.

displayName.description: Human-readable reviewer name shown in management UI and comments.

reviewFacet.description: Review facet label produced by this agent.

modelTier.description: Semantic Zeeq model tier resolved through organization LLM settings at runtime.

prompt.description: Reviewer-specific instructions appended to the shared review prompt.

enabled.description: Whether the persisted agent can participate in future reviews.

activationConfiguration.description: File activation filters that decide whether this agent runs for a PR.

CodeReviewerAgentDto.description: Persisted reviewer-agent configuration returned to management screens.

items.description: Persisted reviewer agents for the repository.

agent.description: Persisted reviewer-agent configuration after the mutation.

CodeReviewerAgentResponse.description: Response for a single persisted reviewer-agent mutation.

key.description: Stable template identifier used for selection and telemetry.

displayName.description: Human-readable persona name shown in the library.

description.description: Short summary describing when to reach for this persona.

modelTier.description: Semantic Zeeq model tier the persona defaults to.

prompt.description: Reviewer instructions seeded into a new agent's prompt.

activationConfiguration.description: Default file activation rules for the persona.

CodeReviewerAgentTemplateDto.description: A code-defined, clonable reviewer-agent template surfaced in the management UI.

items.description: Clonable reviewer-agent templates, in display order.

CodeReviewerAgentTemplateListResponse.description: Response for the built-in reviewer-agent template catalog.

includedFiles.description: Allowlist rules. Empty means all candidate files are allowed.

excludedFiles.description: Deny rules that always win over includes.

matchType.description: How the pattern should be interpreted.

pattern.description: Repository-relative path, prefix, extension, or glob pattern.

CodeReviewFileNameMatchType.description: Match operation used by a code-review file filter criterion.

line.description: Optional file line for inline findings.

side.description: Optional GitHub diff side, such as RIGHT or LEFT.

codeReviewRecordId.description: Stable code review record id that owns these findings.

noAgentsActivated.description: True when configured reviewer agents existed but none matched the reviewed files.

reviews.description: Reviewer facet blocks parsed from the findings artifact.

pullRequestCreatedAtUtc.description: Pull request record creation timestamp and partition key.

codeReviewRecordId.description: Stable code review record id that changed.

codeReviewCreatedAtUtc.description: Review creation timestamp and partition key.

criticalFindings.description: Number of critical findings on the review.

majorFindings.description: Number of major findings on the review.

minorFindings.description: Number of minor findings on the review.

suggestionFindings.description: Number of suggestion-level findings on the review.

commentFindings.description: Number of informational comment findings on the review.

remainingReviewBudget.description: Remaining review budget after this review was accepted.

updatedAtUtc.description: Most recent time this review row was updated.

items.description: Minimal review updates in ascending update order.

oneOf.description: Cursor for polling after the last update in this response.
Cursor for the newest update in this response.

outcome.description: Durable outcome of the request attempt.

commentKind.description: Immediate GitHub comment render kind that was published.

pullRequest.description: Pull request row used for the request.

oneOf.description: Created or referenced code review row, when the outcome has one.

CodeReviewManualRequestResponse.description: Response for manually requesting a new review for a selected pull request.

CodeReviewMissedQueryDto.description: A search that returned zero rows — the content-gap signal.

CodeReviewModelTier.description: Zeeq model quality tier requested by a code reviewer agent.

maxConcurrentReviews.description: Maximum number of code reviews that may run concurrently for the organization.

executionLeaseDurationMinutes.description: Execution lease duration in whole minutes.

CodeReviewPullRequestDetailResponse.description: Response for one selected pull request.

createdAtUtc.description: Pull request record creation timestamp and partition key.

updatedAtUtc.description: Most recent time this pull request row was updated.

organizationId.description: Organization that owns the pull request row.

teamId.description: Team associated with the pull request row, when any.

repositoryId.description: Configured Zeeq repository id for this pull request.

ownerQualifiedRepoName.description: Provider-qualified repository name, such as owner/repo.

headSha.description: Current pull request head commit SHA.

authorLogin.description: Provider login for the pull request author.

htmlUrl.description: Browser URL for the pull request.

isDraft.description: True when the pull request is currently a draft.

state.description: Current provider pull request lifecycle state.

claimStatus.description: Current Zeeq claim state for review assignment.

claimedByUserId.description: Zeeq user id that claimed the pull request, when claimed.

featureId.description: Optional Zeeq feature id associated with the pull request.

lastWebhookAtUtc.description: Most recent webhook time observed for this pull request.

singleViewToken.description: Compact URL-safe token encoding (CreatedAtUtc, mode=Pr) for the Mode 1 share link.

checkRunBlocking.description: True when the PR has an active blocking check run.

CodeReviewPullRequestDto.description: Pull request row returned to the code-review inbox.

items.description: Pull request rows in newest-first order.

oneOf.description: Cursor for loading the next older page, when available.
Cursor for the newest row in this response, when available.
Initial cursor for polling minimal review updates for this inbox window.

CodeReviewPullRequestListResponse.description: Response for the PR inbox list.

items.description: Review rows for the selected pull request in newest-first order.

oneOf.description: Cursor for loading the next older page, when available.
Cursor for the newest row in this response, when available.

CodeReviewPullRequestReviewListResponse.description: Response for one selected pull request's review history.

createdAtUtc.description: Review creation timestamp and partition key.

updatedAtUtc.description: Most recent time this review row was updated.

pullRequestRecordId.description: Stable pull request record id reviewed by this execution. Null for agent (MCP) reviews.

repositoryId.description: Configured Zeeq repository id for this review. Null for agent reviews without a resolved repository.

ownerQualifiedRepoName.description: Provider-qualified repository name, such as owner/repo.

pullRequestNumber.description: Provider pull request number reviewed by this execution.

branch.description: Source branch reviewed by this execution.

title.description: Pull request title captured when the review was created.

authorLogin.description: Provider login for the pull request author.

requestOrigin.description: Source that requested this review execution.

reviewGroupId.description: Optional group id tying related review attempts together.

remainingReviewBudget.description: Remaining review budget after this review was accepted.

criticalFindings.description: Number of critical findings produced by the review.

majorFindings.description: Number of major findings produced by the review.

minorFindings.description: Number of minor findings produced by the review.

suggestionFindings.description: Number of suggestion-level findings produced by the review.

commentFindings.description: Number of informational comment findings produced by the review.

findingsStorageUri.description: Storage URI for the full review findings artifact, when available.

failureMessage.description: Failure text for errored reviews, when available.

hasSourceTelemetry.description: Hint that a non-empty source-telemetry payload is stored and fetchable via the findings endpoint; the findings endpoint remains the source of truth for the actual content.

CodeReviewRecordDto.description: Review row returned for one selected pull request.

fileFilter.description: Repository-level file filter applied before agent activation.

oneOf.description:
Check-run gating settings for this repository.
Omitted or null means the feature is disabled (backward compatible).

sharedPromptFragment.description:
Shared prompt fragment injected into every reviewer agent's prompt for
this repository. Omitted or null means no organization-wide guidance
is added (backward compatible).

CodeReviewRepositoryConfigurationDto.description: Repository-level review configuration stored in typed JSONB.

CodeReviewRepositoryConfigurationRespons.description: Response for reading repository-level review configuration.

CodeReviewRequestOrigin.description: Source that requested a code review run.

CodeReviewRequestOutcome.description: Durable outcome category for a code review request attempt.

facet.description: Reviewer facet label, such as Security or Performance.

findings.description: Actionable findings emitted by this reviewer.

CodeReviewReviewerFindingsDto.description: Findings and summary text emitted by one reviewer facet.

CodeReviewSingleViewMode.description: Access mode for the single code-review view.

review.description: The primary review the link targets (expanded first in the UI).

reviews.description: Related set to render, newest-first, primary included.

oneOf.description: The reviewed pull request, when the review is tied to one.

CodeReviewSingleViewResponse.description: Response for the single code-review deep-link view.

readAfterSearch.description: Whether the doc was searched and later read (relevance proxy).

facets.description: Distinct reviewer facets that surfaced this document.

bestRank.description: Min 1-based rank across search arms; 0 when never ranked.

bestScore.description: Max fused relevance score across hits.

snippets.description: Snippets surfaced within this document, ordered by importance.

CodeReviewSourceDocumentDto.description: One consulted document and the snippets surfaced within it.

language.description: Fence language for code samples; null for sections.

hitCount.description: Number of hits that surfaced this snippet.

facets.description: Distinct reviewer facets that surfaced this snippet.

bestRank.description: Min 1-based rank across search arms; 0 when never ranked.

bestScore.description: Max fused relevance score across hits.

identifierMatch.description: Whether any contributing hit had an identifier overlap.

queries.description: Distinct queries that surfaced this snippet.

CodeReviewSourceSnippetDto.description: One snippet (prose section or code sample) surfaced within a document.

schemaVersion.description: Storage schema version the payload was produced with.

CodeReviewSourceTelemetryDto.description:
Readable API projection of the review's source telemetry (which KB documents/snippets the
reviewers consulted, tool usage, and content-gap misses).

CodeReviewStatus.description: Execution status for a code review run.

createdAtUtc.description: Partition timestamp for the last row in the page.

id.description: Stable row id used as the cursor tie-breaker.

CodeReviewStreamCursorDto.description: DTO cursor for streams ordered newest first by `CreatedAtUtc, Id`.

CodeReviewToolUsageDto.description: Per-tool invocation counts observed during a review run.

reviewCreatedAtLowerBoundUtc.description: Review creation timestamp lower bound used for partition pruning.

updatedAtUtc.description: Update timestamp high-water mark for the polling stream.

createdAtUtc.description: Review creation timestamp tie-breaker for updates with the same update time.

id.description: Review row id tie-breaker for updates with the same update and creation timestamps.

teamId.description: Effective team filter that produced this cursor, when any.

repositoryId.description: Effective repository filter that produced this cursor, when any.

scope.description: Effective inbox scope that produced this cursor.

subjectUserId.description: Server-derived user id for mine-scoped cursors, when applicable.

CodeReviewUpdateCursorDto.description: DTO cursor for inbox review update polling.

displayName.description: Human-readable reviewer name shown in management UI and comments.

reviewFacet.description: Review facet label produced by this agent.

modelTier.description: Semantic Zeeq model tier resolved through organization LLM settings at runtime.

prompt.description: Reviewer-specific instructions appended to the shared review prompt.

enabled.description: Whether the persisted agent can participate in future reviews.

activationConfiguration.description: File activation filters that decide whether this agent runs for a PR.

CreateCodeReviewerAgentRequest.description: Request for creating a repository reviewer agent.

role.description: Organization role to grant when the invitation is accepted.

CreateInvitationRequest.description: Request body for inviting a user to the current organization.

name.description: Human-readable library name, unique in the active organization.

oneOf.description:
Present only when creating a repository-sourced library. Absent means
a plain hand-authored ("local") library, as before this field existed.

CreateLibraryRequest.description: Request body for creating a document library.

kind.description: Which kind of repository source to create.

repoUrl.description:
Raw GitHub clone URL. Required and only used when
LibrarySourceKindRequest CreateLibrarySourceRequest.Kind is LibrarySourceKindRequest.Public.

repositoryId.description:
Id of one of the organization's configured GitHub repositories
(`ICodeRepositoryStore`). Required and only used when
LibrarySourceKindRequest CreateLibrarySourceRequest.Kind is LibrarySourceKindRequest.Private.

includeFilters.description: Org-level include path globs. Empty means include everything.

excludeFilters.description: Org-level exclude path globs. Empty means exclude nothing.

CreateLibrarySourceRequest.description: Source configuration for creating a repository-backed library.

CreateLlmApiKeyRequest.description: Request body for creating a tenant-owned LLM API key.

CreateOrganizationRequest.description: Request body for creating an organization.

title.description: Display title resolved from the markdown source.

keywords.description: Normalized keywords derived from front matter.

tokenCount.description: Estimated token count for the searchable content.

excludedFromCodeReviews.description:
True when the document is hidden from list/search results on the code-review execution path.
Drives the editor's exclusion toggle state; always false for remote documents (v1).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document was last updated.

DocumentContentResponse.description: Full document including markdown body, for the editor load path.

DocumentError.description: Error response for document endpoint validation failures.

title.description: Resolved title: front-matter title → first H1 → file name.

keywords.description: Normalized keywords derived from front matter.

headings.description: Plain heading text, in document order.

snippets.description: Composed section and code snippets, in document order.

DocumentParsePreviewResponse.description:
Preview of what the parse and snippet-indexing pipeline would extract from a document's
current content — the title/keywords/headings that would be stored, and the section/code
snippets that would be composed for secondary indexing. Nothing is persisted; this runs the
same pure parse/compose steps the write path uses, on demand.

language.description: Fence language (code only); null for section snippets.

tag.description: Resolved fence tag (code only); null for section snippets.

identifiers.description: Lowercased extracted identifiers (code only); empty for sections.

tokenCount.description: Token count of the snippet's embedding payload.

DocumentParsePreviewSnippetResponse.description:
One section or code snippet the indexing pipeline would compose from a document's current
content. Mirrors ComposedSnippet, minus the
embedding payload/hash — this is a read-only preview, not the persisted indexing shape.

title.description: Display title resolved from the markdown source.

keywords.description: Normalized keywords derived from front matter.

tokenCount.description: Estimated token count for the searchable content.

excludedFromCodeReviews.description:
True when the document is hidden from list/search results on the code-review execution path
(operational/informational content reviewers must not consult). Always false for remote
documents — v1 scopes exclusion to hand-authored documents.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document was last updated.

DocumentResponse.description: API response for a library document.

library.description: The library name this document belongs to.

title.description: Display title resolved from the markdown source.

keywords.description: Normalized keywords derived from front matter.

DocumentSearchResultResponse.description:
A ranked search hit for the "Test" panel. `Library` is echoed from the API so each
row carries its owning library name independently (D-1).

GitHubCreateRepositoryMappingRequest.description: Request to register a GitHub repository for Zeeq code review.

GitHubUpdateRepositoryMappingRequest.description: Request to update local management fields on an existing repository mapping.

GitHubUpdateRepositoryVisibilityRequest.description: Request to control whether an installation-visible repository appears as a library source.

type.description:
A URI reference [RFC3986] that identifies the problem type. This specification encourages that, when
dereferenced, it provide human-readable documentation for the problem type
(e.g., using HTML [W3C.REC-html5-20141028]). When this member is not present, its value is assumed to be
"about:blank".

status.description: The HTTP status code([RFC7231], Section 6) generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem. It may or may not yield further information if dereferenced.

errors.description: Gets the validation errors associated with this instance of HttpValidationProblemDetails.

kind.description: Type of event represented by this record.

event.id.description: Optional stable event identifier from the source harness.

event.timestamp.description: Optional timestamp for when the event occurred.

prompt_text.description: Prompt text for prompt events, when capture is enabled.

mcp.server.name.description: MCP server name for MCP-backed tool calls.

gen_ai.tool.call.success.description: Whether the tool call completed successfully.

gen_ai.request.model.description: Model name for prompt or completion events.

gen_ai.usage.input_tokens.description: Total input token count for completion events.

gen_ai.usage.output_tokens.description: Output token count for completion events.

gen_ai.usage.reasoning_tokens.description: Reasoning token count for completion events.

user.email.description: Observed source user email. This does not authorize the request.

organization_id.description: Observed source organization id. This does not authorize the request.

ImportedAgentEvent.description: One event in a direct telemetry import request.

IngestError.description: Error payload for ingest trigger endpoints.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor for the next page, or `null` when this page
is the last one (fewer rows than the requested limit were returned).

IngestRunPageResponse.description: One page of a library's ingest run history, newest first.

IngestRunSummaryResponse.description: Summary of one ingest run for the run-history list.

organizationName.description: Display name of the inviting organization.

createdAtUtc.description: UTC timestamp when the invitation was created.

expiresAtUtc.description: UTC timestamp when the invitation expires.

InvitationResponse.description: Pending organization invitation returned by membership endpoints.

LibraryError.description: Error response for library endpoint validation failures.

duplicateLocalPaths.description: Local paths that require overwrite confirmation.

blockedRemotePaths.description: Synced/remote paths that cannot be overwritten by import.

LibraryImportConflictResponse.description: Conflict response for import attempts that require explicit user action.

documentCount.description: Number of package documents that would be imported.

newPaths.description: Paths that do not currently exist in the target library.

duplicateLocalPaths.description: Existing local paths that would be overwritten.

blockedRemotePaths.description: Existing synced/remote paths that block import.

LibraryImportPreviewResponse.description: Preview response for importing a signed Zeeq library export package.

updatedPaths.description: Existing local paths overwritten by the import.

LibraryImportResponse.description: Import result for a signed Zeeq library export package.

oneOf.description: Signed Zeeq export package. Plain zip files are intentionally not importable.

LibraryImportUploadRequest.description: Multipart form request for importing a signed Zeeq library export package.

libraryId.description: The stable library ID whose mappings were updated.

repositoryIds.description: The repository IDs currently mapped to the library.

LibraryRepositoryMappingsResponse.description: Response body for the library repository mappings endpoint.

oneOf.description: Present only for a repository-sourced library.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the library was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the library was last updated.

LibraryResponse.description: API response for a document library.

LibrarySourceKindRequest.description: Discriminates which kind of repository source a library create request configures.

repoUrl.description:
The resolved clone URL either way — from the joined `DocsPublicSource`
for a public-source library, or the library's own `SourceRepoUrl` for
a private-source library. Never editable after creation.

nextSyncAt.description: Next scheduled sync time, if any.

lastSyncedAt.description: Last successful sync time, if any.

quarantined.description:
Always `false` for Private. For Public, reflects the
joined `DocsPublicSource.Status == "quarantined"` — the upstream
repository is no longer public; documents are frozen, not deleted.

LibrarySourceResponse.description: Repository-source metadata and sync status for a repository-backed library.

LlmApiKeyResponse.description: Key metadata returned by the API. Plaintext is intentionally omitted.

LlmProviderAccessTestResult.description: Sanitized result for an LLM provider access test.

LlmSettingsConfigurationResponse.description: Organization LLM tier configuration returned to the settings UI.

LlmSettingsError.description: Error response for LLM settings API failures.

LlmSettingsViewResponse.description: Response returned by the organization LLM settings route.

LlmTierSettingsRequest.description: Request body for one tier's provider/model/key selection.

joinedAtUtc.description: UTC timestamp when the member joined.

MemberResponse.description: Organization member displayed in member lists.

MembershipStatus.description: Lifecycle status of an OrganizationMembership row.

MetricLeaderboardItem.description: One ranked leaderboard item (UI-7): a path with its owning library and total.

MetricPercentilePoint.description: One bucket's p50/p95/p99 for a histogram metric (UI-8/UI-9).

metricValue.description: The measured value (for example elapsed ms).

tokens.description: Token count from the `tokens` tag, when present.

MetricScatterPoint.description: One raw sample for a duration-vs-tokens scatter (UI-8/UI-9).

MetricsEndpointError.description: Caller-facing error for the metrics read endpoints.

MetricSeriesGroup.description: Group dimension for a metric series.

seriesKey.description: Group value (user, tool, library, …) or null when ungrouped.

value.description: Summed metric value for the bucket/series.

MetricSeriesPoint.description: One bucketed point in a metric time series, optionally grouped by a single dimension.

MetricsFilterOptions.description: Distinct filter values available across the org's metric + review data.

MetricsOverview.description: Headline stat-card numbers for the overview tab.

MetricsRepositoryOption.description: One selectable repository option for the review filters (id + display name).

value.description: Summed metric value for the bucket/dimension pair.

MetricTwoDimensionalSeriesPoint.description: One bucketed point in a metric time series grouped by two dimensions.

role.description: Current user's role in the organization, when available.

createdAtUtc.description: UTC timestamp when the organization was created.

activatedAtUtc.description: UTC timestamp when the organization became active.

autoInviteSameDomain.description: Normalized domain currently claimed for auto-invites.

autoInviteDefaultRole.description: Role assigned to auto-created same-domain invitations.

autoInviteSameDomainCanEnable.description: Whether the organization can enable same-domain onboarding.

autoInviteSameDomainBlockReason.description: Reason enabling is blocked, when any.

OrganizationResponse.description: Organization details returned by membership endpoints.

type.description:
A URI reference [RFC3986] that identifies the problem type. This specification encourages that, when
dereferenced, it provide human-readable documentation for the problem type
(e.g., using HTML [W3C.REC-html5-20141028]). When this member is not present, its value is assumed to be
"about:blank".

status.description: The HTTP status code([RFC7231], Section 6) generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem. It may or may not yield further information if dereferenced.

ProblemDetails.description: A machine-readable format for specifying errors in HTTP API responses based on <see href="https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7807" />.

PullRequestClaimStatus.description: Current assignment state for a pull request in Zeeq.

PullRequestState.description: Durable state of a pull request as reported by the provider.

fromPath.description: Current document path to move from.

toPath.description: Target document path to move to.

RenameDocumentRequest.description: Request body to rename (move) a document to a new path within the same library (D-3).

RenameLlmApiKeyRequest.description: Request body for renaming an existing tenant-owned LLM API key.

ResetLibraryIngestRunStateResponse.description: Response returned after clearing a private library's active ingest state.

ReviewFindingsPoint.description: Bucketed finding-severity sums, optionally grouped by repository/author (UI-3).

ReviewVolumePoint.description: Bucketed review volume, optionally grouped by repository/author/origin (UI-4/UI-5).

RotateLlmApiKeyRequest.description: Request body for rotating an existing tenant-owned LLM API key.

SameDomainInvitationDetailsResponse.description: Same-domain invitation details shown before a user accepts the invitation.

domain.description: Normalized domain currently claimed for auto-invites.

canEnable.description: Whether the organization can enable same-domain onboarding.

blockReason.description: Reason enabling is blocked, when any.

SameDomainOnboardingStatusResponse.description: Current same-domain onboarding settings and enablement eligibility.

hash.description: Client-computed SHA-256 hex digest of the finding's content.

title.description: Display title for saved-cart metadata and copy-source reconstruction.

criticality.description: Finding criticality/severity, as its string name.

line.description: Optional file line for inline findings.

side.description: Optional GitHub diff side, such as RIGHT or LEFT.

facet.description: Reviewer facet label, such as Security or Performance.

annotation.description:
Optional free-text note the user attached when adding this finding (max
int CartLimits.MaxAnnotationLength chars).

addedAtUtc.description: Timestamp when this finding was added to the (then-draft) cart.

SaveCartItemRequest.description: One full finding snapshot inside an initial save or copy-source payload.

id.description: Client-generated cart id, preserved from the draft.

createdAtUtc.description: When the draft was originally created client-side.

items.description: Full finding payloads to persist for this cart.

SaveCartRequest.description:
The full client-side draft, submitted once on first save. string SaveCartRequest.Id and
string SaveCartRequest.Name were minted client-side when the draft was created and are
preserved unchanged; DateTimeOffset SaveCartRequest.CreatedAtUtc is the draft's original creation
time, not "now".

maxConcurrentReviews.description: Maximum number of code reviews that may run concurrently for the organization.

SaveCodeReviewOrganizationSettingsReques.description: Request for saving organization-level code-review execution limits.

SaveCodeReviewRepositoryConfigurationReq.description: Request for saving repository-level review configuration.

SaveLlmSettingsRequest.description: Request body for saving organization LLM tier settings.

documentId.description: Stable document identifier for the loaded document to update.

excluded.description: True to hide the document from code-review list/search results.

SetDocumentReviewExclusionRequest.description: Request body to set or clear a document's code-review exclusion flag.

SlugCheckResponse.description: Result of checking whether an organization slug can be used.

documentPath.description: The owning document's current normalized path.

headingPath.description: Hierarchical heading path (breadcrumb style, e.g. "Guide > Install > Linux").

header.description: Heading text that owns this snippet.

language.description: Fence language (code snippets only); null for section snippets.

score.description: Fused RRF score (plus identifier boost); higher ranks first.

vectorRank.description: 1-based rank in the HNSW vector arm; 0 if not a vector-arm hit.

textRank.description: 1-based rank in the FTS arm; 0 if not a full-text-arm hit.

identifierMatch.description: Whether the query's extracted identifiers overlapped this row's.

degraded.description: True when semantic ranking was unavailable and this row is FTS-only.

SnippetSearchResultResponse.description: A ranked snippet search hit for the "Test" panel's Sections/Code modes.

TestLlmSettingsRequest.description: Request body for testing provider/model/key access.

runId.description:
The id the eventual `DocsIngestRun` record will use. No row exists
with this id yet — the queued sync message carries it forward so the
consumer creates the row with this exact id once it processes the job.

viewToken.description:
Compact token encoding RunCreatedAtUtc and source kind,
for a future run-status viewer endpoint.

TriggerIngestRunResponse.description: Response returned by a successful manual ingest trigger.

displayName.description: Human-readable reviewer name shown in management UI and comments.

reviewFacet.description: Review facet label produced by this agent.

modelTier.description: Semantic Zeeq model tier resolved through organization LLM settings at runtime.

prompt.description: Reviewer-specific instructions appended to the shared review prompt.

enabled.description: Whether the persisted agent can participate in future reviews.

activationConfiguration.description: File activation filters that decide whether this agent runs for a PR.

UpdateCodeReviewerAgentRequest.description: Request for replacing editable repository reviewer-agent fields.

repositoryIds.description:
IDs of the repositories that should be mapped to this library.
Must be non-deleted repository IDs belonging to the caller's organization.
An empty array clears all mappings.

UpdateLibraryRepositoryMappingsRequest.description:
Request body for updating the set of repositories mapped to a library.
Replaces the full set of repository mappings for this library.

includeFilters.description:
Org-level include path globs. `null` leaves the
existing value unchanged (e.g. when editing a plain local library);
an empty array explicitly clears it. Ignored for a library with no
repository source. Never changes the repository URL/kind — delete
and recreate the library to change the source.

excludeFilters.description: Org-level exclude path globs. Same null-vs-empty semantics as string[]? UpdateLibraryRequest.IncludeFilters.

UpdateLibraryRequest.description: Request body for updating a document library.

UpdateOrganizationRequest.description: Request body for updating organization display details.

enabled.description: Whether same-domain onboarding should be enabled.

defaultRole.description: Role assigned to auto-created invitations; defaults to `member`.

UpdateSameDomainOnboardingRequest.description: Request body for same-domain onboarding settings.

content.description: Full markdown source to parse and persist.

UpsertDocumentRequest.description: Request body for writing a markdown document into a library.

displayName.description: User-facing token name shown in management UI.

expiresInDays.description: Requested lifetime in days, bounded by auth settings.

UserTokenCreateRequest.description: Browser request body for minting a long-lived user-owned MCP bearer token.

id.description: Local user-token metadata row ID embedded in issued tokens.

createdAtUtc.description: Timestamp when the token metadata row was created.

expiresAtUtc.description: Hard expiry for the issued access token.

revokedAtUtc.description: Timestamp when the token was revoked, if any.

lastUsedAtUtc.description: Most recent successful validation timestamp, if any.

UserTokenSummary.description: Non-sensitive metadata for a long-lived user token.
